instruction: Each rule in this list must be adversarial-tested by a DIFFERENT agent than the one that generated it. Assign no rule to its originating worker. Priority order: CRITICAL > HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW.

rules.description: Genotoxicity battery timing corrected: only Ames assay required before Phase I single-dose trials; complete battery required before Phase II. Original rule incorrectly required complete battery before Phase I.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: does the rule correctly allow Phase I to start with only Ames assay complete? Does it correctly block Phase II without complete battery?

rules.description: BCS biowaiver jurisdiction divergence corrected: all three (FDA, EMA, PMDA) now accept Class I AND Class III per ICH M9. Original claimed significant divergence (FDA: Class I only).
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: what happens for BCS Class II compounds? Test: pre-M9 (2019) vs post-M9 positions. Test: are any ICH M9 opt-outs documented for any jurisdiction?
rules.client_financial_risk: $500K-$2M per unnecessary in vivo BE study

rules.description: PBRER annual submission deadline corrected 90 → 70 calendar days per ICH E2C(R2) §2.8.3. 90-day deadline applies only to ad hoc PBRERs.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: 6-month PBRER deadline (70 days). Test: ad hoc PBRER deadline (90 days). Test: what triggers the 90-day window for ad hoc?

rules.domain: EU Paediatric / SPC extension / Orphan exclusivity
rules.description: Article citation corrected: 6-month SPC extension attributed to Art. 36(1), orphan exclusivity to Art. 37 of Reg 1901/2006. Previously both were cited to Art. 37 only.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: are both Art. 36(1) and Art. 37 required to trigger the SPC extension? Test: does Art. 37 alone grant the extension or only in combination?

rules.description: SAE definition restructured from informal 6-item list to 5 formal ICH E2A WHO/ICH criteria plus importance/medical judgement clause.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: is hospitalization alone sufficient to classify as SAE? Test: does 'medically important' as standalone criterion require meeting other criteria simultaneously?

rules.description: PBRER Section 7 title terminology corrected: 'Reporting Period' → 'Reporting Interval' per ICH E2C(R2) §2.9.2.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: is 'reporting period' vs 'reporting interval' substantively different in E2C(R2) context? Test: does this terminology appear in submission templates?

rules.domain: Clinical safety / ICH E19 selective data collection
rules.description: ICH E19 mandatory vs. optional safety data conflation resolved. Separated into mandatory_safety_data and encouraged_safety_data with explicit ICH E19 §2.3 and §2.4 sourcing.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: which items from the original 7-item list are mandatory vs. encouraged? Test: does E19 apply to all late-phase trials or only specified conditions?

rules.description: ICH E6(R3) section citation corrected: monitoring requirements in §3.11.4 (not §3.3).
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: what does §3.3 actually cover in E6(R3)? Test: does §3.11.4 fully specify risk-based monitoring or does it cross-reference other sections?

rules.description: EU +1 year data exclusivity extension corrected: applies to new indications (8+2+1), not pediatric studies (which use separate Reg 1901/2006 mechanism).
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: what exactly qualifies as a 'new indication' for the +1 year? Test: can pediatric approval trigger the +1 year separately from the Paediatric Regulation?

rules.description: FDA RSABE scope corrected: applies to both AUC and Cmax, not AUC only.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: which metrics use RSABE vs. ABE? Test: what are the reference-scaled limits for Cmax under RSABE?

rules.description: New rule: standalone acute toxicity studies are NOT recommended per ICH M3(R2) §4 as a default. Study is not required before Phase I for most small molecules.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: when IS a standalone acute study required? Test: does this apply to biologics? Test: does non-recommendation mean a jurisdiction cannot require it?

rules.description: New rule: lethality as a study endpoint is prohibited in acute toxicity studies. Applies to study intent/design, not unexpected mortality outcomes.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: if animals die during an acute study, does that constitute a prohibited lethality endpoint? Test: how does LD50 estimation align with this prohibition?

rules.description: New rule: standard limit dose is 1000 mg/kg. Exception ceiling of 2000 mg/kg applies when human therapeutic dose exceeds 1g/day. The 50-fold AUC margin is an alternative.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: when exactly does the 2000 mg/kg ceiling apply vs. 1000? Test: can 50-fold AUC always substitute for the mg/kg limit? Test: route-specific limit dose differences?

rules.description: New taxonomy node: PMA device component deficiencies blocking NDA/BLA approval. Detection signal: PMA + Not Approvable letter + CDRH reference.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: apply to NDA 208276 — does it correctly classify as CAT-11-A? Test: can an NDA/BLA with CDRH involvement but no PMA still trigger CAT-11-A?

rules.description: New taxonomy node: postmarketing requirements/commitments under 505(o)(3). Covers 17 CRLs (4.1% of database). Previously uncovered.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: distinguish CAT-09-C from existing CAT-09 nodes. Test: does PMC (commitment) classify differently than PMR (requirement)?

rules.description: Source attribution corrected: S1A-03 'before Phase III' timing is from M3(R2) Table 1, not S1A. S1A contains carcinogenicity study design requirements, not clinical phase timing gates.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: does M3(R2) Table 1 alone govern Phase III timing for carcinogenicity? Test: are there S1A requirements that modify the Table 1 timing?

rules.description: New rule: dose-escalation studies, dose-ranging studies, and early-phase repeat-dose studies can substitute for standalone acute toxicity studies.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: does any one of these substitutes suffice, or is a combination required? Test: does this apply before any Phase I or only certain types?

rules.domain: Acute toxicology / Route restriction and GLP
rules.description: New rule: route must match clinical route; GLP compliance is preferred but not mandatory for acute studies.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: what if clinical route is IV but oral acute data exists? Test: what triggers mandatory GLP for acute tox?

rules.description: New rule: acute toxicity data (or substitute) must be available before Phase III initiation. This is an availability gate, not a standalone study requirement.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: if dose-escalation data exists before Phase III, is the gate met? Test: does the gate apply to biologics under M3(R2)?

rules.description: New rule: microdose exploratory trials (Phase 0, ≤100 µg) are exempt from acute toxicity study requirements.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: what is the exact dose threshold for microdose exception? Test: does the exception apply if Phase 0 is followed immediately by Phase I?

rules.description: New rule: minimum design parameters if study conducted include 14-day observation (OECD-sourced), dose escalation, specified endpoints, GLP preferred, both sexes, matching clinical route.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: is 14-day observation period mandatory per ICH M3(R2) or only OECD? Test: what endpoints are explicitly required vs. encouraged?

rules.description: Japan data exclusivity description corrected: Japan is tied for longest with EU at 10 years, not 'longest' outright.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: are EU 10 years and Japan 10 years calculated from the same starting event? Test: any conditions that extend beyond 10 years in either jurisdiction?

rules.description: FDA retention description corrected to 21 CFR 211.180(a): 1 year after expiry for standard products (not a two-option rule).
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: are there product types where retention period differs from 1-year-after-expiry? Test: does 21 CFR 211.180(a) apply to all NDA product types equally?

rules.description: Broken reference annotated: 21 USC citation confirmed correct via 21 CFR cross-reference and FDA guidance. Annotation added noting statute is outside corpus.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: does the rule content match 21 CFR codification of the 21 USC requirement? Test: any regulatory guidance that modifies the statutory requirement?

rules.description: EU rule numeric threshold corrected with verbatim article reference added.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: verify corrected threshold against original EMA guideline. Test: is there a transitional period where old threshold still applies?

rules.description: EU rule corrected: missing enumeration items added with source article reference.
rules.adversarial_test_hint: Test: are the added enumeration items exhaustive or illustrative? Test: any EU guidance that limits or expands the list?

pending_clarification_items.description: 17 jurisdiction divergences flagged 'needs clarification' by jurisdiction worker. These require dedicated research pass by a different agent before adversarial testing is meaningful.
pending_clarification_items.instruction: Assign to a jurisdiction research agent (NOT the original jurisdiction worker) to resolve ambiguities, then add resolved items to next READY_FOR_ADVERSARIAL list.

deferred_items.description: CRL Non-Proprietary Name/Suffix pattern (1 example only; taxonomy ID conflicts with CAT-09-C in current scheme). Deferred to correction round 2.
